description: One primary company release anchors several syndicated copies.
An unverified rumour is later confirmed by an issuer release.
An issuer denial disproves an earlier rumour.
A primary macro release is revised and the prior value is superseded.
A positive company announcement is followed by a negative stock reaction.
A negative event is followed by a positive stock reaction.
A sourced event has no material move over the supported window.
A primary regulator notice maps directly to a sector and named securities.
One confirmed event explicitly names several registered securities.
A common word that is also a ticker is not resolved without controlled context.
A timezone-aware event falls after the regular close.
A timezone-aware event falls before the regular open.
A Saturday event maps to the next eligible market date without inventing a session reaction.
An old event remains stale and cannot be presented as current.
An event without a valid publication time fails normalization.
The selected production provider is explicitly unavailable with no fixture fallback.
Five-minute bars show a transparent reversal after an event without proving causation.
A complete quiet session has no material event rather than a fabricated catalyst.
Daily OHLCV supports a candle summary but blocks intraday narration.
An active partial session discloses incompleteness and does not narrate the close.
An injected early close changes final-hour and close boundaries.
Different headline wording for one structured event deduplicates to one cluster.
A corrected publisher item retains supersession lineage and does not count twice.
Two sources disagree and the contradiction remains explicit.
A macro release has an actual value but no consensus field.
A bond ETF move remains a proxy and is not stated as a direct yield change.
Price reaction exists while volume confirmation is unavailable.
Saved-symbol overlap affects user relevance but never portfolio impact.
A theme event maps only to supported affected constituents.
Price moved before the event timestamp, so causal attribution is rejected.
Normalize a Zulu publication timestamp.
Normalize a valid numeric timezone offset.
Normalize Unicode without changing factual content.
Collapse presentation whitespace deterministically.
Retain an allowed HTTPS canonical URL.
Reject a javascript URL while retaining safe metadata.
Bound an oversized source summary without storing a body.
Retain a valid BCP-47-style language tag.
Provider-specific fields do not enter the consumer contract.
Reject a malformed provider identifier.
Configured SEC domain is primary for filing events.
Configured Federal Reserve domain is primary for policy events.
Configured government statistics domain is primary for releases.
Configured issuer IR source is primary only for its issuer categories.
Configured exchange notice source is primary for exchange notices.
Configured court notice is primary for the controlled legal category.
Configured professional wire is high-confidence secondary.
Identifiable authored analysis is supporting secondary.
An anonymous claim is unverified.
A lookalike official domain is not upgraded.
Structured central-bank metadata classifies monetary policy.
Structured statistics metadata classifies inflation.
Structured statistics metadata classifies employment.
Issuer result metadata classifies earnings.
Issuer outlook metadata classifies guidance separately from earnings.
Structured transaction metadata classifies M&A.
Offering metadata classifies a capital raise.
Incident metadata classifies a cybersecurity incident.
Officer-change metadata classifies management change.
Insufficient controlled metadata falls back to other without invented subtype.
Exact provider identifiers collapse deterministically.
Cross-source structured event identifiers cluster.
Syndicated normalized headline tokens cluster with matching entity/type/time.
A secondary item referencing the primary URL joins its cluster.
A headline update becomes cluster history, not a new confirmation.
Same entity and time but a different event type stays separate.
Similar text outside the clustering window stays separate.
Similar text for different issuers stays separate.
Later confirmation changes status while preserving rumour lineage.
Duplicate coverage reduces no uncertainty and adds no confirmation.
An explicitly named registered symbol maps directly.
A controlled company alias resolves to its registry symbol.
A direct security maps to its versioned sector membership.
A direct security maps only to reviewed theme memberships.
A registered ETF maps to its benchmark relationship.
A peer relationship is emitted only from a controlled map.
Explicit saved symbols produce watchlist-overlap mappings.
An unregistered supplier relationship is not inferred.
Every mapping retains version, source, evidence, and freshness.
A malformed ticker string is quarantined.
Primary and directly named evidence increases entity materiality transparently.
A broad confirmed policy event increases market materiality.
A supported observed reaction contributes without implying prediction.
Supported volume confirmation has a separate contribution.
Supported breadth confirmation has a separate contribution.
Staleness reduces materiality transparently.
Developing status applies an uncertainty penalty.
Duplicate sources do not inflate the score.
User relevance is separate from global materiality.
All contributions produce a bounded 0-100 score.
A five-minute window is assessed only with covering bars.
A fifteen-minute window is assessed only with covering bars.
Session-to-date output is explicitly partial during an active session.
Completed daily bars support close-to-close reaction.
Two completed daily observations support a next-session window.
A bounded daily series supports an explicit multi-day window.
Excess return uses a date-aligned benchmark observation.
Sector-relative return uses a date-aligned sector proxy.
A reaction stays insufficient when price evidence is missing.
Bars outside the requested window cannot support a reaction claim.
A bar before 09:30 America/New_York is premarket.
The configured opening phase begins at the regular open.
Morning follows the opening phase without overlap.
Midday uses configured exchange-local boundaries.
Afternoon precedes the final hour.
The final hour is relative to the scheduled close.
The close segment is exchange-local and non-overlapping.
Post-close bars are after-hours.
A configured exchange holiday produces a holiday state.
A weekend produces a weekend state without session bars.
Transparent bar rules identify a trend-up session.
Transparent bar rules identify a trend-down session.
Noise-limited bars remain range-bound.
Supporting bars and magnitude identify a morning reversal.
Supporting bars and magnitude identify an afternoon reversal.
A failed recovery retains supporting timestamps.
A failed breakdown retains supporting timestamps.
Close location supports close-near-high.
Approximate intraday VWAP supports reclaim only with covering OHLCV.
Volume pace remains unavailable without same-time history.
Latest market news routes to the existing Market agent and News evidence.
A material-event explanation preserves fact/reaction separation.
Session questions route to Session evidence beneath an existing agent.
Security news routes to the existing Stock agent.
Sector news routes to the existing Sector agent.
Theme news routes to the existing Theme agent.
Macro event reaction routes to the existing Macro agent.
Event detail retains one canonical event and evidence IDs.
Catalyst comparison does not create a new registered agent.
Headline-price contradiction emits both sides.
A provider timeout becomes structured unavailable.
A rate limit becomes structured unavailable with no mock fallback.
Malformed input is quarantined without a replacement headline.
Invalid time fails closed.
Conflicting duplicate IDs preserve a validation error.
A source registry miss remains unverified.
Mapping failure leaves the entity absent and disclosed.
An expired cache is stale, never live.
Corrupted intraday bars block detailed narration.
Missing calendar blocks exchange-session claims.
Instruction text inside a headline is quarantined.
HTML is removed from normalized display text.
Markdown links cannot create trusted sources.
Script content is removed and flagged.
Non-HTTPS and credential-bearing URLs are rejected.
An oversized body is never persisted or traced.
Display-name spoofing cannot override registry identity.
A lookalike domain remains unverified.
A request to reveal provider credentials is refused.
Unsupported causal wording is rejected or rewritten noncausally.
